data.title: Westlake Financial sells $100 million subprime auto loans to HFC Acceptance, completing landmark transaction at 112% premium.
data.description: In Los Angeles, the leading auto finance firm, Westlake Financial, disclosed the lucrative sale of around $100 million in subprime auto loans to HFC Acceptance, LLC. This transaction, named WTTD-2026-1, marks a unique arrangement for both parties, attained at a substantial principal premium of 112%. The deal comprises a mix of established and freshly originated subprime auto finance loans, underlining a successful collaboration between the two entities.

data.title: Nathan Simington appointed Director of Policy & Strategy at Ericsson Federal for U.S. Government telecom.
data.description: Ericsson Federal Technologies Group in Vienna, Virginia, has appointed Nathan Simington as the new Director of Policy & Strategy. With his extensive experience and leadership skills, Simington aims to enhance Ericsson Federal's presence in the U.S. Government and Department of War telecommunications and technology domains. This move is expected to bolster Ericsson Federal's position in the industry. Simington's role will focus on accelerating the adoption and integration of 5G technology within these sectors.

data.title: Tammi Heaton of PrideStaff named to 2026 Staffing 100 North America list for fifth time by SIA.
data.description: In Fresno, California, PrideStaff, a franchised staffing organization, has announced that Co-CEO Tammi Heaton has been recognized on Staffing Industry Analysts' (SIA) 2026 Staffing 100 North America list. This is the fifth consecutive year that she has been included in the prestigious list. The news was shared on January 13, 2026.

data.title: Startup Island TAIWAN Silicon Valley Hub celebrates 1st anniversary, aiding 35+ startups, creating 1000+ business connections.
data.description: A technology innovation hub supported by the NDC has facilitated the growth of over 35 startups and enabled more than 1,000 cross-border business connections. The Startup Island TAIWAN Silicon Valley Hub marked its one-year anniversary on January 12 in Palo Alto, California.

data.title: American Packaging Corporation debuts eco-friendly seasoning packets designed for recyclability. RE™ Design for Recycle High Barrier Paper pouch promotes sustainability. 160 characters.
data.description: In Columbus, Wisconsin, on January 13, 2026, the American Packaging Corporation (APC) unveils the RE™ Design for Recycle High Barrier Paper pouch, aimed at enhancing the shelf presence of seasoning brands. This sustainable packaging option is tailored for companies striving to make an eco-conscious impact.

data.title: Heifer International's 2025 Annual Report highlights farmer leadership in building sustainable food systems, emphasizing partnerships and innovation.
data.description: A recent report highlights the significant impact of partnerships, local leadership, and innovation in enhancing smallholder resilience and rural livelihoods in light of increasing global challenges. Released in Little Rock, Arkansas on January 13, 2026, Heifer International's digital 2025 Annual Report showcases the success of these strategies. Key factors contributing to this success include collaborations, leadership at the local level, and innovative approaches. This report provides valuable insights into how these elements have positively influenced smallholder communities, emphasizing the importance of proactive measures in the face of global uncertainty.

data.title: Arden launches heated cushion seat, partners with USA Curling to support athletes' families by providing comfort.
data.description: In Detroit, Arden, a prominent outdoor furnishings brand under Central Garden & Pet Company, has unveiled a partnership with USA Curling. This collaboration focuses on providing comfort for athletes' families while they support their loved ones. The highlight of the partnership is the introduction of Arden's latest heated, portable stadium cushions. These cushions are complemented by travel aid to facilitate the presence of athletes' families during competitions. Family support is crucial, and this initiative aims to prioritize both comfort and encouragement for the athletes.

data.title: SharpLink Gaming aims to expand in 2026 with $3 billion in Ethereum, showing transparency in utilizing ETH for growth.
data.description: SharpLink Gaming, an Ethereum treasury firm, aims to set a pioneering example for publicly traded companies by effectively utilizing their Ethereum holdings.

data.title: BlueNord ASA reports disclosure of shareholdings by Kite Lake Capital Management (UK) LLP and Taconic Capital Advisors UK LLP on Jan 13, 2026.
data.description: Do not share, publish, or distribute in the United States, Canada, or Japan. Oslo, Norway, Jan. 13, 2026 - Kite Lake Capital Management (UK) LLP ("Kite Lake") and Taconic Capital Advisors UK LLP ("Taconic") announced a joint venture.

data.title: Talon Air, a Long Island-based jet charter company, acquired in private transaction on Jan. 2, 2026, led by CEO James Chitty.
data.description: A Long Island-based jet charter and aircraft fleet management company, Talon Air, was recently acquired in a private transaction that concluded on January 2, 2026. The acquisition was led by the current CEO, James Chitty. This acquisition marks a significant change in ownership for the company, highlighting the dynamic nature of the aviation industry. Talon Air is renowned for its high-quality services and extensive fleet, making it a key player in the industry. The exact financial details of the transaction were not disclosed to the public. This acquisition is expected to have far-reaching implications for Talon Air's operations and market positioning moving forward.

data.title: Dr. Kimberly Biss appointed to ACIP, joining IMA Senior Fellow Dr. Kirk Milhoan and Senior Advisor Dr. Robert Malone on vaccine advisory panel.
data.description: In a recent announcement on January 13, 2026, Dr. Biss has become a new addition to the Vaccine Advisory Panel of the Independent Medical Alliance (IMA). This panel now includes esteemed members Dr. Kirk Milhoan as Senior Fellow and Dr. Robert Malone as Senior Advisor. IMA, a prominent coalition comprising independent physicians, healthcare professionals, and researchers, aims to provide valuable insights into the realm of healthcare. This development further strengthens the panel, bringing a wealth of experience and knowledge to the table. The expertise of these three individuals will significantly benefit the alliance's efforts in fostering advancements in the medical field.

data.title: Townsend in Danvers, MA, emphasizes propane tank safety after Littleton incident with evacuations and road closure. #SafetyFirst
data.description: In Danvers, Massachusetts, on January 13, 2026, it was reported that there was a propane leak incident in Littleton, New Hampshire. As a result, evacuations were carried out, and roads had to be closed off. In response to this event, Townsend issued a reminder to all residents and businesses about the vital significance of adhering to proper propane tank filling protocols. It serves as a critical precautionary measure to prevent similar incidents in the future, emphasizing the need for stringent safety measures when handling propane.

data.title: UAE holds over 400 MW power capacity in GCC data center market dominated by Saudi Arabia, hosting 110+ data centers.
data.description: Dublin - A new database titled "GCC Existing & Upcoming Data Center Portfolio" has recently been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's collection. The upcoming data center market in the GCC is heavily influenced by Saudi Arabia, holding nearly 60% of the total power capacity. The GCC region currently accommodates over 110 operational data centers, with notable presence in countries like the UAE and Saudi Arabia. The United Arab Emirates leads the existing market with a power capacity exceeding 400 MW, thanks to key industry players in the region.

data.title: Tickets available for Life Fest event on Jan. 23 in Oxon Hill, Maryland, organized by Knights of Columbus and Sisters of Life for pre-March for Life rally; thousands expected to attend.
data.description: A significant event is scheduled to take place on January 23, 2026, in Oxon Hill, Maryland. The Knights of Columbus and Sisters of Life are joining forces once more to organize a pre-March for Life rally called Life Fest. This event is expected to bring together thousands of individuals for a powerful gathering. The rally will feature live performances and various activities as part of the event lineup.
